However, semantically 'Rule 1.16(e)' (digit 1) makes more sense than 'Rule l.16(e)' (letter l). Flagging as high-risk citation element that should be human-verified against the rendered PDF.

description: OCR corruption artifact in PDF source: '-N^' appears in the PDF pdftotext output immediately before footnote 1. This is garbage text that resulted from OCR processing of a footnote marker in the original PDF. The database successfully filtered this out during ingestion. This artifact is in the PDF source, not a DB divergence, and is appropriately handled by the separate footnote/markup processing pipeline per Rules 6 and 11.
Section heading 'HI' appears (should be 'III' based on contextual numbering: I, II, III, IV, V). This appears to be a heading numbering error - the section uses Roman numeral II earlier and IV later.

description: Markup contamination: asterisks around case citations throughout opinion. Multiple instances of markdown italics formatting (*...*) around case names in the database text that do not appear in the source PDF. Examples: '*Combs v. Lund,*', '*Hanisch v. Kroshus,*', '*see also*', '*District of Columbia v. Heller,*', '*McDonald v. City of Chicago, Ill.,*', '*Heller,*', '*State v. Ricehill,*', '*See City of Bismarck v. Schoppert,*' and others. Per rule 11, markup contamination is handled by a separate deterministic pass; these artifacts are not proposed as fixes but flagged for awareness.

Sanders case reference contains multiple defects: (1) italics markup asterisks (*In re Sanders,*) treated as content rather than formatting; (2) page marker *881 embedded within citation; (3) parallel citation '108 N.M. 434' present in DB but absent from authoritative PDF source. High-risk citation element requiring manual review.

location_quote: DB: In re Sanders, 108 N.M. 434, 773 P.2d 1241 (N.M. Ct. App. 1989) | PDF: In re Sanders, 773 P.2d 1241, 1245 (N.M. Ct. App. 1989)
description: Discrepancy in Sanders parallel citation: DB includes New Mexico Reporter reference (108 N.M. 434) which does not appear in authoritative PDF. This is a high-risk citation element with potential OCR hallucination (false parallel citation added during ingestion). The page marker *881 interrupting the DB version suggests corruption during text extraction. Flagged for manual verification before any citation-based decision.

location_quote: [¶1] Angela Alimón appeals from a judgment granting her a divorce from Aaron Alimón, granting her primary residential responsibility for their child, ordering him to pay child support, and distributing their marital property.
description: CRITICAL: Party name spelling is corrupted systematically throughout the document. The database contains 38 instances of 'Alimón' (with accent mark), 4 instances of 'Ali-món' (hyphenated with accent), and 1 instance of 'All-mon' (hyphenated without accent), totaling 43 incorrect forms. The PDF source uses 'Allmon' (no accent, no hyphen) consistently in all 64 instances. This is a systematic OCR or character encoding corruption. The correct party names are 'Angela R. Allmon' (plaintiff/appellant) and 'Aaron D. Allmon' (defendant/appellee). Only 16 instances in the DB have the correct spelling. This represents a critical data integrity issue affecting the parties' names throughout the opinion.

The document contains 6 page reference markers (*872, *873, *874, *875, *876, *877) from the Northwestern Reporter citation system. These are expected legal formatting elements and should be retained as they appear in the official PDF. No action needed.
